name: coyote
description: Worklog and task management MCP server for Coyote — log work, query tasks, and manage issues directly from Claude.

tools.name: coyote_login
tools.description: Start Coyote authentication. Opens a browser to the verification page and returns the code to enter there. After completing authentication in the browser, call coyote_login_complete.

tools.name: coyote_login_complete
tools.description: Complete Coyote authentication after the user has approved the request in the browser. Call this after coyote_login once the browser authentication is done.

tools.name: coyote_get_me
tools.description: Return the currently authenticated Coyote user.

tools.name: coyote_update_me
tools.description: Update the current user's profile (name and/or title).

tools.name: coyote_upgrade
tools.description: Check for a new version of Coyote MCP and download it if available. Call this when any Coyote tool returns a 500 error, as that often indicates a version incompatibility between the MCP and the Coyote API.

tools.name: coyote_list_issues
tools.description: List issues, optionally filtered by project, sprint, owner, status, or keyword.

tools.name: coyote_get_issue
tools.description: Get full details of an issue by its slug (e.g. CHR-5).

tools.name: coyote_create_issue
tools.description: Create a new issue in a sprint. Strongly prefer setting start_date and end_date — they place the issue on the Timeline/Gantt view. If you omit both, they default to the parent sprint's dates.

tools.name: coyote_update_issue
tools.description: Update an existing issue by slug.

tools.name: coyote_delete_issue
tools.description: Delete an issue by slug. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_list_tasks
tools.description: List tasks, optionally filtered by owner, issue, project, sprint, status, or keyword.

tools.name: coyote_get_task
tools.description: Get full details of a task by its slug (e.g. CHR-T1). Response includes associated activities and derived phases.

tools.name: coyote_create_task
tools.description: Create a new task under an issue. Strongly prefer setting start_date and end_date — they place the task on the Timeline/Gantt view. If you omit both, they default to the parent issue's dates.

tools.name: coyote_update_task
tools.description: Update an existing task by slug.

tools.name: coyote_delete_task
tools.description: Delete a task by slug. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_list_worklogs
tools.description: List worklogs, optionally filtered by user, task, project, date range, or keyword.

tools.name: coyote_get_worklog
tools.description: Get full details of a worklog by its slug (e.g. CHR-W3). Times are returned in the caller's local TZ.

tools.name: coyote_create_worklog
tools.description: Record work time on a Coyote task. `date` / `start_time` / `end_time` are interpreted as the caller's local time and stored as UTC alongside the recorder's offset. `start_time` is required and must be the actual work start — the MCP does not default it to the current clock.

tools.name: coyote_update_worklog
tools.description: Update an existing worklog by slug. When `date` / `start_time` / `end_time` are provided they are interpreted as local time and converted to UTC. If any of those are touched, the worklog is normalized to UTC storage even if it was a legacy row.

tools.name: coyote_delete_worklog
tools.description: Delete a worklog by slug. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_list_projects
tools.description: List all Coyote projects accessible to the current user.

tools.name: coyote_list_members
tools.description: List members of a project.

tools.name: coyote_get_project
tools.description: Get details of a single Coyote project by ID.

tools.name: coyote_create_project
tools.description: Create a new project. Requires system admin role.

tools.name: coyote_update_project
tools.description: Update a project. Requires system admin role.

tools.name: coyote_delete_project
tools.description: Delete a project. Requires system admin role.

tools.name: coyote_list_sprints
tools.description: List sprints for a project, optionally filtered by type or keyword.

tools.name: coyote_create_sprint
tools.description: Create a new sprint. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_update_sprint
tools.description: Update an existing sprint. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_delete_sprint
tools.description: Delete a sprint. Requires project admin role.

tools.name: coyote_get_sprint
tools.description: Get details of a single sprint by ID.

tools.name: coyote_list_categories
tools.description: List categories for a project.

tools.name: coyote_create_category
tools.description: Create a new category in a project. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_update_category
tools.description: Update a category. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_reorder_categories
tools.description: Reorder all categories within a project. Pass the category IDs in the desired order. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_delete_category
tools.description: Delete a category. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_list_phases
tools.description: List phases for a project.

tools.name: coyote_create_phase
tools.description: Create a new phase in a project. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_update_phase
tools.description: Update a phase. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_reorder_phases
tools.description: Reorder all phases within a project. Pass the phase IDs in the desired order. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_delete_phase
tools.description: Delete a phase. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_list_patterns
tools.description: List patterns, optionally filtered by category or level.

tools.name: coyote_get_pattern
tools.description: Get full details of a pattern by ID.

tools.name: coyote_create_pattern
tools.description: Create a new work pattern template.

tools.name: coyote_update_pattern
tools.description: Update a pattern by ID.

tools.name: coyote_delete_pattern
tools.description: Delete a pattern by ID.

tools.name: coyote_list_activities
tools.description: List activities, optionally filtered by project, phase, or type.

tools.name: coyote_create_activity
tools.description: Create a new activity. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_get_activity
tools.description: Get full details of an activity by ID.

tools.name: coyote_update_activity
tools.description: Update an activity. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_delete_activity
tools.description: Delete an activity. Requires project admin or manager role.

tools.name: coyote_add_member
tools.description: Add a user to a project. Requires project admin role.

tools.name: coyote_update_member_role
tools.description: Change a project member's role. Requires project admin role.

tools.name: coyote_remove_member
tools.description: Remove a user from a project. Requires project admin role.

tools.name: coyote_list_vendors
tools.description: List all vendors. Useful for resolving vendor IDs when creating or filtering issues.

tools.name: coyote_create_vendor
tools.description: Create a new vendor. Requires system admin role.

tools.name: coyote_update_vendor
tools.description: Update a vendor. Requires system admin role.

tools.name: coyote_delete_vendor
tools.description: Delete a vendor. Requires system admin role.

tools.name: coyote_list_users
tools.description: List all users. Useful for resolving user IDs when assigning task owners or issue owners.

tools.name: coyote_get_user
tools.description: Get details of a user by ID.

tools.name: coyote_update_user
tools.description: Update a user. Requires system admin role.

tools.name: coyote_deactivate_user
tools.description: Deactivate a user (sets active=0 and removes from all projects). Requires system admin role.

tools.name: coyote_reactivate_user
tools.description: Reactivate a deactivated user (sets active=1). Requires system admin role.
